Notable_Events: First Space Shuttle flight; first winged reusable spacecraft to orbit and return; first crewed maiden voyage of a US orbital vehicle
First reuse of a crewed orbital vehicle; first operation of RMS (Canadarm); mission shortened from 5 to 2 days due to fuel cell failure
First landing at White Sands New Mexico (Northrup Strip); thermal tests of orbiter
Final orbital test flight; first DoD payload; landed on concrete runway at Edwards AFB; President Reagan attended landing
First operational mission; first deployment of commercial satellites; first 4-person crew; planned EVA cancelled due to spacesuit malfunction
Challenger's maiden flight; first Shuttle EVA (Musgrave & Peterson); TDRS-1 deployed (IUS malfunction placed it in wrong orbit initially)
First American woman in space (Sally Ride); first 5-person crew; first satellite deployed and retrieved (SPAS-01)
First African-American in space (Guion Bluford); first night launch; first night landing
First Spacelab mission; first 6-person crew; first non-US astronaut on Shuttle (Ulf Merbold ESA); Young's 6th and final spaceflight
First untethered spacewalk (Bruce McCandless with MMU); first Shuttle landing at KSC; two satellites failed to reach proper orbit
First in-orbit satellite repair (Solar Maximum Mission); first direct ascent trajectory; deployed LDEF
Discovery's maiden flight; first flight of PAM-D II; deployed 3 communications satellites; first commercial payload specialist (Charles Walker)
First 7-person crew; first mission with two women (Ride & Sullivan); first American woman EVA (Kathryn Sullivan); first Canadian in space (Marc Garneau)

Primary_Payload: Anik D-2 / Syncom IV-1 / Palapa B-2 & Westar 6 retrieval
Notable_Events: First satellite retrieval mission; recovered Palapa B-2 and Westar 6 (failed satellites from STS-41-B); first time satellites returned to Earth
First dedicated DoD mission; classified payload; shortest Discovery mission

Primary_Payload: Anik C-1 / Syncom IV-3 / Unscheduled EVA
Notable_Events: Syncom IV-3 (Leasat) failed to activate after deploy; unscheduled EVA to attempt repair (unsuccessful); first sitting US Senator in space (Jake Garn)
Second dedicated Spacelab mission; materials science and fluid physics experiments; carried two squirrel monkeys and 24 rats

Primary_Payload: Morelos-A / Arabsat 1-B / Telstar 3-D / SPARTAN-101 / SDI experiment
Notable_Events: First Arab astronaut (Sultan bin Salman); first French astronaut on Shuttle (Patrick Baudry); deployed 3 commsats; SDI High Precision Tracking Experiment
Abort to Orbit (ATO) — first in-flight engine shutdown and only ATO in Shuttle history; Spacelab 2 solar/stellar astronomy

Primary_Payload: ASC-1 / Aussat 1 / Syncom IV-4 / Syncom IV-3 repair
Notable_Events: Deployed 3 communications satellites; repaired Syncom IV-3 (Leasat) during EVA that STS-51-D could not fix
Atlantis maiden flight; second dedicated DoD mission; deployed two DSCS-III satellites
Largest crew ever launched (8 people); first ESA-managed Spacelab mission; German D-1 Spacelab; Challenger's final successful mission
First Mexican-American in space (Rodolfo Neri Vela); deployed 3 commsats; EASE/ACCESS construction experiments
Last successful mission before Challenger disaster; first sitting US Congressman in space (Bill Nelson); future NASA Administrator Charles Bolden was pilot
Challenger disaster: vehicle broke apart 73 seconds after launch; all 7 crew killed; O-ring failure in right SRB; Teacher in Space Christa McAuliffe aboard
Return to Flight after Challenger disaster (32-month stand-down); deployed TDRS-C successfully
Classified DoD mission; severe TPS tile damage discovered post-flight (over 700 tiles damaged); nearly catastrophic reentry
Deployed TDRS-D; IMAX camera experiments
First planetary probe deployed from Shuttle; Magellan radar-mapped Venus 1990-1994
Classified DoD mission; deployed SDS-2 intelligence relay satellite
Deployed Galileo spacecraft to Jupiter; second planetary probe from Shuttle; Galileo arrived Jupiter 1995
Classified DoD mission; fifth night launch
Retrieved LDEF (deployed on STS-41-C in 1984 after 5.7 years in orbit); longest mission to date at time
Classified DoD mission; highest inclination (62 deg) Shuttle mission from KSC
Deployed Hubble Space Telescope; one of the most significant Shuttle missions; HST still operational 35+ years later
Deployed Ulysses solar polar orbiter (ESA/NASA); third planetary probe from Shuttle; highest energy Earth-departure ever at time
Classified DoD mission
ASTRO-1 ultraviolet astronomy telescopes; multiple delays before launch; Broad Band X-Ray Telescope and Hopkins Ultraviolet Telescope
Deployed Compton Gamma Ray Observatory (2nd of NASA's Great Observatories); unplanned EVA to free stuck antenna
Unclassified DoD mission; infrared and ultraviolet background measurements for SDI; largest unclassified DoD payload
First dedicated Spacelab Life Sciences mission; extensive human physiology research; last Columbia flight to land at Edwards AFB
Deployed TDRS-E; 5th TDRS satellite
Deployed UARS to study ozone depletion and atmospheric chemistry; UARS operated until 2005

Primary_Payload: DSP-16 (Defense Support Program satellite) / Terra Scout
Notable_Events: Deployed DSP early-warning satellite; mission shortened by 3 days due to IMU failure; carried military Terra Scout experiment
First International Microgravity Laboratory; first Canadian woman in space (Roberta Bondar); 55 experiments from 16 countries

Primary_Payload: ATLAS-1 (Atmospheric Laboratory for Applications and Science)
Notable_Events: First ATLAS mission; 12 instruments studying atmospheric science and solar observations; first Belgian astronaut (Dirk Frimout)
Endeavour's maiden flight; first 3-person EVA; captured and repaired Intelsat 603; longest EVA to date (8h 29m); first 4 EVAs on single mission
First US Microgravity Laboratory; first Extended Duration Orbiter (EDO) pallet; longest Columbia mission at time
First Tethered Satellite System test (tether jammed at 840 ft of planned 12.5 mi); deployed ESA EURECA retrievable carrier; first Italian/Swiss crew
First Japanese Spacelab mission; first African-American woman in space (Mae Jemison); first married couple in space (Lee and Davis)
Deployed LAGEOS II geodetic satellite (NASA/ASI); USMP-1 microgravity experiments
Last dedicated DoD Shuttle mission; last classified Shuttle payload
Deployed TDRS-F; DXS (Diffuse X-ray Spectrometer) experiment; EVA test of assembly techniques
Second ATLAS atmospheric science mission; free-flying SPARTAN-201 solar observatory
Second German Spacelab mission; nearly 90 experiments in materials/life science; longest Columbia mission to that point
First SPACEHAB commercial module flight; retrieved ESA EURECA (deployed on STS-46)
Deployed ACTS advanced comms satellite; first EVA to test HST repair tools (for STS-61); ORFEUS-SPAS astronomy
Second dedicated life sciences Spacelab; longest Shuttle mission at that time; 14 experiments on human adaptation to microgravity
First HST servicing mission; installed COSTAR corrective optics and WFPC2; fixed Hubble's flawed mirror; 5 EVAs totaling 35+ hours; saved the telescope
First Russian cosmonaut on Shuttle (Sergei Krikalev); beginning of US-Russian cooperation
Microgravity and technology experiments; longest Columbia mission to date
First Space Radar Laboratory mission; three imaging radars mapped Earth's surface and environment
Second International Microgravity Laboratory; first Japanese woman in space (Chiaki Mukai); longest Shuttle mission at time; 82 experiments
First test of SAFER (jet backpack for EVA rescue); LITE first space-based lidar system; SPARTAN solar wind studies
Second Space Radar Lab mission; mapped Earth again for comparison with SRL-1 seasonal changes
Third ATLAS atmospheric science mission; studied middle atmosphere chemistry; CRISTA infrared observations
First Shuttle-Mir rendezvous (flyaround approach to 37 ft); first female Shuttle pilot (Eileen Collins); first cosmonaut on Discovery
ASTRO-2 ultraviolet astronomy; longest Shuttle mission at time (16 days); 3 UV telescopes observed 300+ celestial objects
First Shuttle-Mir docking; 100th US human spaceflight; exchanged crew (brought up Mir-19 crew); first time Shuttle-station crew transfer
Deployed TDRS-G (7th and final first-generation TDRS); woodpecker delayed launch by damaging ET foam
Second free-flight of SPARTAN-201; Wake Shield Facility grew thin film semiconductors in ultra-vacuum of wake
Second US Microgravity Laboratory; fluid physics crystal growth and combustion experiments; 16-day mission
Second Shuttle-Mir docking; attached Russian-built Docking Module to Mir's Kristall; module used for all subsequent Shuttle-Mir dockings
Retrieved Japanese Space Flyer Unit (SFU); tested EVA tools planned for ISS assembly; deployed and retrieved OAST-Flyer
Tethered Satellite System reflight — tether deployed to 12.2 mi but snapped; satellite lost; demonstrated tether electrodynamics before failure

Primary_Payload: SPACEHAB-SM / Mir docking 3 / Shannon Lucid transfer
Notable_Events: Third Shuttle-Mir docking; transferred Shannon Lucid to Mir for 188-day stay; first EVA during docked Shuttle-Mir mission
Inflatable Antenna Experiment (IAE) — first large inflatable structure in orbit; SPACEHAB commercial experiments
Life and Microgravity Spacelab; longest Shuttle mission at time; 40+ experiments from 10 countries
Fourth Shuttle-Mir docking; exchanged crew (Blaha for Lucid); first SPACEHAB Double Module
Longest Shuttle mission ever (17d 15h); deployed and retrieved ORFEUS-SPAS II and WSF-3; planned EVAs cancelled (jammed airlock hatch)
Fifth Shuttle-Mir docking; exchanged crew (Linenger for Blaha); transferred 6 tons of supplies and experiments
Second HST servicing; installed STIS and NICMOS instruments; 5 EVAs totaling 33+ hours; greatly expanded Hubble's capabilities
Mission cut short from 16 to 4 days due to fuel cell problem; reflown as STS-94; shortest mission since return-to-flight
Sixth Shuttle-Mir docking; exchanged crew (Foale for Linenger); transferred supplies to Mir
Reflight of STS-83 payload; successfully completed full-duration microgravity research; same crew as STS-83
CRISTA-SPAS-2 atmospheric measurements; tested Japanese robotic arm technology (precursor to ISS JEM RMS)
Seventh Shuttle-Mir docking; exchanged crew (Wolf for Foale); first EVA by a Russian from the Shuttle (Vladimir Titov)
Fourth flight of SPARTAN-201; USMP-4 materials science; first EVA by a woman outside the Shuttle (Kalpana Chawla controlled RMS inside); Leonid Kadenyuk (Ukraine)
Eighth Shuttle-Mir docking; exchanged crew (Thomas for Wolf); SPACEHAB experiments
Neurolab — last dedicated Spacelab mission; 26 neuroscience experiments studying the nervous system in microgravity; last science-dedicated Columbia mission
Ninth and final Shuttle-Mir docking; last US astronaut returned from Mir (Andrew Thomas); AMS-01 prototype (alpha magnetic spectrometer); end of Shuttle-Mir program
John Glenn returned to space at age 77 (oldest person in space); first Spanish astronaut (Pedro Duque); Hubble Orbiting Systems Test
First ISS assembly mission; connected Unity (Node 1) to Russian Zarya module; first Shuttle crew to enter ISS; start of ISS construction
First Shuttle docking with ISS; delivered supplies and equipment; installed crane and portable foot restraints; Canadarm used for ISS work
First female Shuttle commander; deployed Chandra X-ray Observatory (3rd of NASA's Great Observatories); electrical short during ascent caused main engine controller issues
Third HST servicing; replaced all 6 gyroscopes; new computer; new Fine Guidance Sensor; 3 EVAs; urgent mission due to gyro failures threatening HST
Shuttle Radar Topography Mission mapped 80% of Earth's land surface in 3D; 200-ft radar mast deployed from payload bay; most topographic data ever collected
ISS repair and logistics mission; replaced and repaired equipment on ISS; one EVA; Atlantis became 2nd orbiter to visit ISS
Prepared ISS Zvezda Service Module for first crew (Expedition 1); installed batteries converters and supplies; one EVA to connect cables between Zarya and Zvezda
100th Shuttle mission; attached Z1 truss (first ISS truss element) and PMA-3; 4 EVAs; prepared ISS for first permanent crew
Installed P6 ITS solar arrays — first ISS solar power module; provided electrical power for ISS; 3 EVAs; visited Expedition 1 crew
Delivered and installed US Destiny Lab — ISS primary research facility; 3 EVAs; key milestone in ISS construction
First MPLM (Leonardo) flight to ISS; Expedition 1/2 crew exchange; transferred 5 tons of supplies via MPLM; 2 EVAs
Installed Canadarm2 (ISS robotic arm); first MPLM Raffaello flight; Chris Hadfield first Canadian to walk in space; 2 EVAs
Installed Quest Joint Airlock — enabled EVAs directly from ISS with both US and Russian suits; 3 EVAs during installation
Third Leonardo MPLM flight; Expedition 2/3 crew exchange; 2 EVAs; delivered science experiments and supplies
Expedition 3/4 crew exchange; Raffaello MPLM delivered supplies; one EVA for ISS thermal blanket installation; last flight of 2001
Fourth HST servicing; installed Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS); replaced solar arrays and Power Control Unit; 5 EVAs; significantly upgraded Hubble
Installed S0 (S-Zero) Truss — center backbone segment of ISS truss structure; first use of ISS Canadarm2 to install Shuttle payload; 4 EVAs

Primary_Payload: MBS / Leonardo MPLM / Expedition 4-5 crew rotation
Notable_Events: Installed Mobile Base System on Canadarm2 rail; Leonardo MPLM; Expedition 4/5 crew exchange; 3 EVAs
Installed S1 truss segment (starboard side); first use of CETA cart on ISS truss; 3 EVAs
Installed P1 truss segment (port side); Expedition 5/6 crew exchange; 3 EVAs; last Shuttle mission before Columbia disaster
Columbia disaster: orbiter broke apart during reentry over Texas; all 7 crew killed; foam strike during launch damaged wing leading edge thermal protection; Ilan Ramon first Israeli astronaut

Primary_Payload: Raffaello MPLM / ISS logistics / Return to Flight
Notable_Events: Return to Flight after Columbia disaster (29-month stand-down); tested new safety procedures; Raffaello MPLM; first spacewalk repair of Shuttle TPS tiles; foam still shed from ET
Second Return-to-Flight test mission; Independence Day launch; Leonardo MPLM resupply; Thomas Reiter became first ESA long-duration ISS crew
Resumed ISS assembly after Columbia; installed P3/P4 truss and second set of solar arrays; 3 EVAs; restored ISS to full power growth configuration
Installed P5 truss spacer; rewired ISS from temporary to permanent power system; Expedition crew exchange (Reiter returned/S. Williams stayed); 4 EVAs
Installed S3/S4 truss segment and third set of solar arrays; retracted P6 solar array; Expedition crew exchange; 4 EVAs
Installed S5 truss spacer; first educator-astronaut in space (Barbara Morgan — selected after Christa McAuliffe); 4 EVAs; tile damage from debris
Installed Harmony (Node 2) — enabled attachment of European and Japanese labs; second female commander; P6 solar array torn during retraction (repaired by EVA)
Installed ESA Columbus laboratory — Europe's first permanent orbital research facility; Expedition crew exchange; 3 EVAs
Installed Japanese Kibo Experiment Logistics Module (pressurized section) and Canadian Dextre robot; longest Shuttle-ISS docked mission; 5 EVAs
Installed Kibo Japanese Pressurized Module — largest ISS module; completed main Kibo laboratory assembly; 3 EVAs

Primary_Payload: Leonardo MPLM / LWAPA / ISS habitability upgrade
Notable_Events: Delivered equipment for expanding ISS to 6-person crew; new crew quarters water recycling galley exercise equipment; 4 EVAs; prepared ISS for Expedition expansion
Installed S6 truss and fourth (final) set of solar arrays — completed ISS primary power grid; station reached full power capability; 3 EVAs
Fifth and final HST servicing; installed WFC3 and COS instruments; repaired STIS and ACS; new batteries gyros and thermal blankets; 5 EVAs; extended Hubble life through 2030s+
Installed Japanese Exposed Facility and Experiment Logistics Module (exposed section) on Kibo; completed Kibo assembly; 5 EVAs; 13-person ISS crew (largest ever)
Delivered science racks and supplies via Leonardo MPLM; COLBERT treadmill; MARES muscle research facility; Expedition crew exchange; 3 EVAs
Delivered two ExPRESS Logistics Carriers with spare parts for ISS (pumps gyros tanks); 3 EVAs pre-positioning spares for post-Shuttle era
Installed Tranquility (Node 3) and Cupola observation dome — iconic 7-window Earth-viewing module; 3 EVAs
Multi-Purpose Logistics Module Leonardo resupply with 8 racks of science and provisions; 3 EVAs; replaced ammonia tank assembly
Delivered and installed Russian Mini-Research Module Rassvet (MRM-1); also carried Integrated Cargo Carrier; 3 EVAs; last planned Atlantis mission (later extended by STS-135)
Delivered Leonardo Permanent Multipurpose Module (permanently attached to ISS) and ExPRESS Logistics Carrier 4; Robonaut 2 humanoid robot; Discovery's 39th and final flight; 2 EVAs
Installed Alpha Magnetic Spectrometer (AMS-02) — $2B cosmic ray particle physics experiment; ELC-3; 4 EVAs; Endeavour's 25th and final flight; commander Mark Kelly (husband of Gabby Giffords)
Final Space Shuttle mission; smallest crew since STS-6 (4 people); Raffaello MPLM delivered year's worth of supplies; Atlantis' 33rd and final flight; end of 30-year Space Shuttle program
